jokes.text: 20 minutes a day, all feet, no hands and I'll have the pededexterity of a chimp and you'll be sitting there like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Dwight is explaining some kind of foot-based training regimen
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'pededexterity' and comparing himself to a chimp while calling Jim an idiot
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - inventing words ('pededexterity') and bizarre training schemes. Strong character integration and absurd confidence.

jokes.text: Allow me to write you an apology letter. D-e-a... oops, space, 'a. Dear...'
jokes.setup: Dwight offers to write an apology letter with his feet
jokes.punchline: He struggles with basic spelling and typing, narrating his mistakes
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from Dwight's foot-typing experiment. Good cringe factor but predictable outcome.

jokes.text: thank you, hands. Nothing else in the universe can do what you do.
jokes.setup: Dwight fails at foot-typing
jokes.punchline: He gives up and thanks his hands with overly dramatic gratitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - treating hands like they're sentient beings. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Well, 'a' for effort, right?
jokes.setup: Dwight has just failed at his foot-typing demonstration
jokes.punchline: Jim makes a pun referencing both the grade 'A' and the letter 'a' that Dwight struggled to type
jokes.explanation: Clever callback pun - 'A for effort' but literally referencing the 'a' Dwight couldn't type. Perfect Jim response.

jokes.text: Du blueberry, por favor.
jokes.setup: Andy is ordering from what appears to be a food cart
jokes.punchline: He mixes languages unnecessarily - 'du' (German) with 'por favor' (Spanish)
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy pretentiousness but not particularly clever. Functional character moment.

jokes.text: $8? (reaction to muffin price)
jokes.setup: Andy orders what seems like a simple muffin
jokes.punchline: His shock at the ridiculous price
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about overpriced food. Gets a reaction but not memorable.

jokes.text: Owning a building is a war between the landlord and the tenant. Not a literal war, unfortunately.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains his landlord philosophy
jokes.punchline: He's disappointed it's not a literal war
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - takes a metaphor literally and wishes for actual violence. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: And by green, I mean money.
jokes.setup: Dwight mentions his 'green initiative'
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means money, not environmental concerns
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun on 'green.' Good economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: China. China. (Michael's repetitive fixation)
jokes.setup: Michael has discovered something about China
jokes.punchline: His single-word repetitive obsession
jokes.explanation: Sets up Michael's obsession for the episode. More setup than punchline but good character work.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's where they make stuff.
jokes.setup: Michael is shocked everything is made in China
jokes.punchline: Pam's matter-of-fact response deflating his discovery
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response from Pam. Simple but effective deflation of Michael's panic.

jokes.text: China is a sleeping dragon that is just beginning to stir.
jokes.setup: Michael is explaining his fears about China
jokes.punchline: He uses an overwrought, clichéd metaphor with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Michael using tired clichés as profound insights. Fits character but predictable.

jokes.text: China should be like eight.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his worldview ranking system
jokes.punchline: His arbitrary and nonsensical ranking of China as eighth
jokes.explanation: Michael's completely arbitrary ranking system shows his childish understanding of geopolitics.

jokes.text: You gotta stop texting me so much. But I wanted you to know that Michael and I are wearing the same tie today. It's insane.
jokes.setup: Darryl complains about Andy's excessive texting
jokes.punchline: Andy's example of 'important' information is completely trivial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - completely missing social cues and over-sharing trivial details.

jokes.text: B.T.B. Bring that Booty.
jokes.setup: Darryl reminisces about when only desirable people texted
jokes.punchline: His acronym for a crude response
jokes.explanation: Darryl's smooth confidence contrasted with crude abbreviation. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Is that not good enough for your anus?
jokes.setup: Stanley complains about thin toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Dwight's inappropriately direct and crude response
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Dwight response. Cringe factor and direct character voice work well.

jokes.text: Don't get me started on how coddled the modern anus is.
jokes.setup: Dwight's crude toilet paper comment
jokes.punchline: He has strong opinions about anus coddling throughout history
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - treating toilet paper as a historical/cultural issue. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 'Which is you' is not a sentence.
jokes.setup: Stanley says 'Which is you' about the building owner
jokes.punchline: Dwight's grammatical correction while avoiding the point
jokes.explanation: Dwight's pedantic deflection while clearly guilty. Good character moment but predictable.

jokes.text: Hey, Pam, Dwight's being questioned by the police in connection with a string of dognappings...
jokes.setup: Dwight tells Nate to make up an excuse for why he's busy
jokes.punchline: Nate creates an increasingly specific and absurd criminal scenario
jokes.explanation: Nate's overly creative lie-making. Good escalation and absurdity, though Nate isn't as established.

jokes.text: So much for keeping our secrets up high.
jokes.setup: Michael learns the tallest man in the world is Chinese
jokes.punchline: He thinks America's strategy was literally hiding secrets at high altitudes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly stupid Michael logic - treating 'high' literally in terms of national security. Great absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: Where forks are irrelevant.
jokes.setup: Michael describes a world where America isn't number one
jokes.punchline: His completely random fear about forks becoming obsolete
jokes.explanation: Michael's random apocalyptic fear about utensils. Absurd but fits his panicked worldview.

jokes.text: You never told us to close them.
jokes.setup: Michael says 'Now, open your eyes' after his dramatic speech
jokes.punchline: Stanley points out they never closed their eyes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley deflation of Michael's dramatic moment. Great straight-man comedy.

jokes.text: An antacid that you only take once a week.
jokes.setup: Michael asks for big ideas to beat China
jokes.punchline: Kevin's mundane and illogical pharmaceutical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Kevin's typically simple-minded contribution. Not brilliant but fits character.

jokes.text: It's too big a pill to swallow.
jokes.setup: Discussion about yearly antacid pills
jokes.punchline: Creed makes an unintentional pun about the concept being hard to accept
jokes.explanation: Nice accidental double meaning from Creed. Both literal and metaphorical interpretation work.

jokes.text: What if we all get together and help each other and hire a new guy and then we all kill him but first we take out, like, a $100,000 life insurance policy?
jokes.setup: Michael asking for big ideas to save America
jokes.punchline: Creed casually proposes elaborate murder-for-profit scheme
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed darkness delivered casually. The escalation from teamwork to murder is perfectly executed and completely in character.

jokes.text: Suck it, Oscar.
jokes.setup: Michael is proven right about China having more million-population cities
jokes.punchline: His childish victory celebration over Oscar
jokes.explanation: Michael's immature gloating fits character perfectly. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: I will not work in a roach billboard.
jokes.setup: Dwight has put up an exterminator billboard with roaches
jokes.punchline: Angela's horrified reaction to working under giant roach imagery
jokes.explanation: Angela's predictable but fitting disgust reaction. Visual gag works well with her character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I've been playing zombie soccer for two hours?
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Ryan has been
jokes.punchline: He's shocked to discover he's been playing a ridiculous mobile game for hours
jokes.explanation: Ryan's millennial time-wasting habits. Fits character and relatable modern behavior.

jokes.text: Is the nail place Koreans or whites? Koreans. Good. And the dry cleaners? White? Good.
jokes.setup: Discussion about amenities near potential new office
jokes.punchline: Creed's casual racist stereotyping about service quality
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Creed racism delivered matter-of-factly. Fits character but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: He really does fit that old stereotype of the smug gay Mexican.
jokes.setup: Michael explains Oscar's know-it-all tendencies
jokes.punchline: Michael creates a completely nonsensical stereotype
jokes.explanation: Michael's oblivious bigotry creating imaginary stereotypes. Cringe factor high, character-accurate.

jokes.text: You're one bad text away from gettin' blocked. Yes, but one good text away from a high five.
jokes.setup: Darryl threatens to block Andy's texts
jokes.punchline: Andy sees the upside - potential for high five
jokes.explanation: Andy's eternal optimism and desire for approval. Good character dynamic with Darryl.

jokes.text: I wish for a million wishes. Yeah, no, I'm not a genie. Then see you later, building.
jokes.setup: Pam asks Kevin what would make the building awesome
jokes.punchline: Kevin's childish genie reference and immediate dismissal when corrected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic and immediate giving up. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: And 25 years from now, Cece will become world famous for stripping.
jokes.setup: Dwight predicts consequences if Jim's family moves
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and inappropriate prediction about baby Cece's future
jokes.explanation: Dwight's darkly specific and inappropriate prediction. Shocking but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And my daughter, Cece, dances on your grave fully clothed.
jokes.setup: Jim counters Dwight's dark prediction
jokes.punchline: Turns Dwight's stripping prediction into wholesome grave dancing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim comeback - takes Dwight's inappropriate prediction and turns it wholesome while still being dark. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: What do you know about boobs? Michael, I have to tell you something. It's from rocky ii.
jokes.setup: Michael thinks he can discuss boobs to throw off Oscar
jokes.punchline: Jim's question is actually quoting Rocky II, not about anatomy
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up expectation then reveals movie reference. Good structure and surprise.

jokes.text: I'm Pam. Oh. No, you're not. I'm sorry, we have a colleague with the same name. You're not a liar too, are you?
jokes.setup: Michael meets realtor named Pam after discovering office photos were fake
jokes.punchline: His immediate hostility and assumption about lying based on name alone
jokes.explanation: Michael's irrational anger displacement onto innocent person. Good cringe factor and character consistency.

jokes.text: I can't wait to do to Pam what I just did to Pam.
jokes.setup: Michael just yelled at realtor Pam
jokes.punchline: His confusion over having two Pams creates awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Michael's grammatical confusion creates unintentionally threatening statement. Good awkwardness.

jokes.text: I lied about some aspects of the building. It's still on a bike path, though, right? There's no building. It doesn't exist.
jokes.setup: Pam admits to lying about office details
jokes.punchline: Jim's hope for one true detail dashed - nothing exists
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Pam's deception. Jim's diminishing hope creates good comedic structure.

jokes.text: I wasn't here. What? I wasn't here. It's a pretty common saying.
jokes.setup: Michael is losing the China debate with Oscar
jokes.punchline: He pretends not being present is a common conversational escape
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate escape attempt disguised as wisdom. Perfect character panic response.

jokes.text: That as long as people like you and me don't stop talking... Nobody can stop the U.S.A.
jokes.setup: Michael attempts profound conclusion to China debate
jokes.punchline: His completely nonsensical connection between conversation and national power
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at inspirational nonsense. Perfectly captures his pseudo-profound speaking style.

jokes.text: if you dig long enough and hard enough in a conversation, you get to a friend.
jokes.setup: Michael makes metaphor about digging to China
jokes.punchline: His forced parallel about conversational digging leading to friendship
jokes.explanation: Michael's forced metaphor and fake wisdom. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: they say the best vampires don't bleed their victims dry, but give them the strength so that they can bounce back only to be fed on again.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains his landlord philosophy
jokes.punchline: He compares himself to a strategic vampire feeding on tenants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight darkness and twisted business philosophy. Original metaphor executed with complete seriousness.

jokes.text: I was not motivated by compassion. I have no compassion. Make sure you got that. Not motivated by compassion.
jokes.setup: Dwight wants to appear ruthless after showing mercy
jokes.punchline: His over-insistence on lack of compassion while talking to camera operator
jokes.explanation: Dwight's insecurity about appearing soft. The repetition and direct address to camera work well.

jokes.text: Come to parking lot. Crazy pigeon action.
jokes.setup: Darryl's new texting standards after Andy's complaints
jokes.punchline: His idea of worthy news is mundane pigeon activity
jokes.explanation: Good callback to texting subplot. Darryl's misunderstanding of what's text-worthy is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: That's a text. Yeah, right? That's a new standard.
jokes.setup: Andy and Darryl see the pigeons
jokes.punchline: They both agree this mundane event meets the new texting threshold
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution to the texting subplot. Both characters finding common ground in low standards.
